steps.title: Set the shifted function equal to zero
steps.content: The $x$-intercept of the graph of $y=f(x)+6$ occurs when $y=0$. So solve $$f(x)+6=0.$$ Since $f(x)=3d(26x+27)+18$, this becomes $$3d(26x+27)+24=0.$$

steps.title: Solve for the x-coordinate
steps.content: Expand and isolate $x$: $$78dx+81d+24=0$$ $$78dx=-81d-24$$ $$x=\frac{-81d-24}{78d}.$$ Therefore the intercept is $$\left(\frac{-81d-24}{78d},0\right).$$

steps.title: Check with a sample value in Desmos
steps.content: Because $d$ is positive, you can test with an easy value such as $d=1$. Then graph $$y=3(26x+27)+24.$$ Its $x$-intercept is $x=\frac{-105}{78}$, which is exactly what choice B gives when $d=1$: $$\frac{-81(1)-24}{78(1)}=\frac{-105}{78}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A has the wrong sign on $24$. Choice C treats the vertical shift like a horizontal change. Choice D incorrectly puts the added $6$ in the denominator. Only choice B matches the intercept.
